title: veratrum album subsp. oxysepalum
intro: Veratrum album subsp. oxysepalum, also known as white hellebore, is a subspecies of the plant Veratrum album, native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Veratrum album subsp. oxysepalum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it should be handled with caution due to its toxic properties.
